summary: The semantic color and shared glass-component refactor is substantial, but the feature still does not satisfy VAL-DS-007 because Files and Work swipe actions do not expose the required actions; automated coverage also only exercises two helper functions.

issues.title: Required Files and Work swipe actions were not implemented
issues.description: The feature claims VAL-DS-007, which requires trailing swipe actions for Files (delete/rename), Work (archive/pin), and PRs (open in GitHub/close). PR rows have open/close actions, but Files rows only copy path/name and Work rows only copy the lane name or close a running session, so the fulfilled assertion is still unmet.
issues.suggestedFix: Implement the required swipe affordances (Files delete/rename, Work archive/pin) by wiring them to host-backed actions, or remove VAL-DS-007 from this feature's fulfilled assertions until those actions exist.

issues.title: Automated coverage does not exercise the new design-system behaviors
issues.description: The added tests only cover file-icon mapping and file-size formatting. They do not verify any of the new shared glass components, empty/skeleton states, tab minimization, or swipe-action behavior, so regressions in the design-system overhaul will rely on manual inspection.
issues.evidence: apps/ios/ADETests/ADETests.swift:965-976; handoff verification records build/test plus grep checks for ADEPalette, preferredColorScheme(.dark), sensoryFeedback, empty-state/skeleton usage, and tabBarMinimizeBehavior.
issues.suggestedFix: Add focused tests around the shared design-system helpers and at least one view-level regression check for the required swipe-action and empty-state wiring.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `.factory/skills/ios-worker/SKILL.md` is internally inconsistent: line 73 says not to launch the simulator or take screenshots, but lines 95-98 still show simulator-based `interactiveChecks` in the example handoff.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedChange: Update the ios-worker skill so the example handoff matches the procedure: either remove the simulator-based `interactiveChecks` example or change the verification guidance if simulator interaction is actually expected.
sharedStateObservations.reason: This is systemic worker guidance. The contradiction can push workers toward the wrong verification pattern even when they otherwise follow the skill.
